sections.paragraph:
- Original: ὄψίος

- Transliteration: Opsios

- Phonetic: op'-see-os

- Definition:

1. late

2. evening

a. either from three to six o'clock p.m.

b. from six o'clock p.m. to the beginning of night

- Origin: from G3796

- TDNT entry: None

- Part(s) of speech: Adjective

- Strong's: From G3796; late; feminine (as noun) afternoon (early eve) or nightfall (later eve): - even (-ing [-tide]).
